**Allach-Untermenzing** (German pronunciation: [[ˈalax ʔʊntɐˈmɛntsɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Allach-Untermenzing.ogg "File:De-Allach-Untermenzing.ogg"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Allach-Untamenzing*) is the 23rd borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany").

## Allach

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Allach-Untermenzing&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Allach")]

Situated in extreme northwest of the city, the borough consists of the municipalities of Allach and Untermenzing (lit. 'Lower [Menzing](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Menzing "de:Menzing")'). Allach was first documented on March 30, 774 as *Ahaloh*. The name means "forest by the water", where "aha" means water and "loh" means forest. Over time, "loh" became "lach". Allach is one of the oldest independent municipalities in Bavaria. Politically and regionally it was connected to [Dachau](/wiki/Dachau,_Bavaria "Dachau, Bavaria").
